pairs.question: What were the key political positions held by Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed in Sri Lanka?
pairs.answer: Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed served as the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Sri Lanka twice: from 1977 to 1989 and from 1993 to 1994. He also held the position of Minister of Justice & Higher Education from 1989 to 1993.

pairs.question: Which electoral districts did Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed represent during his political career?
pairs.answer: Hameed was first elected from the Akurana electorate in the March 1960 general election. In 1970, he switched to the Harispattuwa electorate and continued to represent it in parliament until his death in 1999.

pairs.question: What notable distinction did Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed achieve as Minister of Foreign Affairs?
pairs.answer: In 1978, Hameed became the first person to hold the position of Minister of Foreign Affairs as a standalone role, rather than it being held by the Prime Minister, which had been the case since 1948. He remains the longest-serving Foreign Minister in Sri Lanka's history.

pairs.question: What educational institutions did Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed attend during his early life?
pairs.answer: Hameed was educated at St. Anthony's College in Katugastota, Vijay College in Matale, and Zahira College in Matale.

pairs.question: Which political party did Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed belong to, and when did he join it?
pairs.answer: He joined the United National Party in 1956 and remained a member until his death.

pairs.question: What academic honors were awarded to Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed?
pairs.answer: Hameed received honorary doctorates from Hankuk University and the University of Sri Jayawardenepura.

pairs.question: How many general elections did Abdul Cader Shahul Hameed contest and win consecutively?
pairs.answer: He contested and won the general elections consecutively from 1960 until his death in 1999, serving as a member of parliament for nearly four decades without interruption.
